input.title: Social Media Application
input.description: Social Media Application is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React Native and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Node.js, Firebase for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: User Registration and Authentication
requirements: Users can register with email or social media accounts
Password must be securely hashed
System sends verification email after registration
Social login via Google and Facebook

FR-02.title: User Profile Creation
requirements: Users can create profile with name, bio, and profile picture
Profile picture supports JPEG and PNG up to 5MB
Username must be unique and 3-20 characters
Bio is optional with max 160 characters

FR-03.title: Post Creation and Sharing
requirements: Users can create posts with text, images, and videos
Posts can be public or private
Users can tag other users in posts
Posts support hashtags for discoverability

FR-04.title: News Feed
requirements: Display posts from followed users in chronological order
Infinite scroll loading for feed
Pull-to-refresh for latest posts
Show post author, content, like count, and comment count

FR-05.title: Like and Comment System
requirements: Users can like and unlike posts
Users can comment on posts
Comments support text and emoji
Post owner receives notification on like and comment

FR-06.title: Follow and Unfollow
requirements: Users can follow other users
Users can unfollow users they follow
Follower and following counts displayed on profile
Private accounts require follow approval

FR-07.title: Direct Messaging
requirements: Users can send private text messages
Messages delivered in real-time
Users can block and report abusive messages
Read receipts show delivery status

FR-08.title: Story Feature
requirements: Users can post stories that disappear after 24 hours
Stories support images and short videos
Stories show viewer list to the author
Stories appear at top of the feed

FR-09.title: User Search and Discovery
requirements: Search bar to find users by name or username
Explore page showing trending posts
Suggested users based on mutual connections
Search results show user avatar, name, and follower count

FR-10.title: Push Notifications
requirements: Notifications for likes, comments, follows, and messages
Users can customize notification preferences
Notification center shows all recent activity
Badge count on app icon

FR-11.title: Content Moderation
requirements: Users can report inappropriate content
Admin dashboard for reviewing reports
Automatic content filtering for profanity
Admin can remove posts and ban users

FR-12.title: Privacy Settings
requirements: Users can set account to private
Users can control who can message them
Users can hide online status
Users can download their data

FR-01.title: Design User Registration and Authentication UI
FR-01.description: Design the user interface for user registration and authentication. Create the screens, forms, dashboards, or status views needed by the SRS and make the workflow clear, responsive, and accessible for the target users.
acceptance_criteria: UI design for user registration and authentication is complete and reviewable.
The user registration and authentication design is responsive and accessible.
The design for user registration and authentication aligns with the documented user flow and validation states.

FR-01.title: Frontend Implementation of User Registration and Authentication
FR-01.description: Implement the frontend behavior for user registration and authentication. Build the required user interactions, validation states, loading states, and error handling so the interface supports these behaviors: Users can register with email or social media accounts; Password must be securely hashed; System sends verification email after registration.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend implements the required user registration and authentication user interactions.
Validation, loading, and error states for user registration and authentication are handled in the UI.
Frontend behavior for user registration and authentication is integrated correctly with backend APIs.

FR-01.title: Backend Implementation of User Registration and Authentication
FR-01.description: Implement the backend logic for user registration and authentication. Build the required APIs, business rules, validation, permissions, persistence, and processing needed to support these behaviors: Users can register with email or social media accounts; Password must be securely hashed; System sends verification email after registration.
acceptance_criteria: Backend logic for user registration and authentication is implemented and testable.
APIs and business rules for user registration and authentication enforce validation and security requirements.
Data handling for user registration and authentication is reliable and supports the required workflow.

FR-01.title: Security Hardening for User Registration and Authentication
FR-01.description: Implement the security controls required for user registration and authentication. Cover authentication, authorization, encryption, auditing, abuse prevention, and compliance checks where the SRS or workflow calls for them.
acceptance_criteria: Security controls for user registration and authentication enforce the required authentication, authorization, or data-protection rules.
Sensitive operations and failure scenarios for user registration and authentication are logged, validated, and protected against misuse.
The implementation of user registration and authentication satisfies the relevant privacy, audit, or compliance expectations in the SRS.

FR-01.title: Integrate User Registration and Authentication with External Services
FR-01.description: Integrate user registration and authentication with the external services or providers referenced by the SRS. Implement secure service connections, payload validation, error handling, and recovery behavior for any third-party dependency involved in the workflow.
acceptance_criteria: External or cross-system integrations required by user registration and authentication exchange the expected payloads successfully.
Retry, timeout, and failure handling for user registration and authentication integrations are implemented and observable.
Credentials, tokens, and outbound calls used by user registration and authentication are configured and secured correctly.

FR-01.title: Testing User Registration and Authentication Functionality
FR-01.description: Create and execute tests for user registration and authentication. Cover the main success flows, failure scenarios, permission checks, validation rules, integrations, and operational edge cases described in the SRS so the implementation is safe to release.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the main success and failure scenarios for user registration and authentication.
Security, validation, and permission checks for user registration and authentication are verified.
Regression coverage exists for the most important user registration and authentication behaviors.

Sensitive operations and failure scenarios for privacy settings are logged, validated, and protected against misuse.
The implementation of privacy settings satisfies the relevant privacy, audit, or compliance expectations in the SRS.

FR-12.title: Testing Privacy Settings Functionality
FR-12.description: Create and execute tests for privacy settings. Cover the main success flows, failure scenarios, permission checks, validation rules, integrations, and operational edge cases described in the SRS so the implementation is safe to release.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the main success and failure scenarios for privacy settings.
Security, validation, and permission checks for privacy settings are verified.
Regression coverage exists for the most important privacy settings behaviors.
